comment: Pydantic models are used for request validation and response schemas, e.g., src/api/admin/course_run/schemas.py and src/api/courses/schemas.py.
Alembic migration scripts are present in alembic/versions and alembic/env.py configures migrations.
Global exception handlers are registered in src/app.py via add_exception_handler for ApplicationError and IncorrectUUIDError.
Dependency injection is performed with FastAPI Depends throughout the project, e.g., src/api/auth/dependencies.py.
Middleware is added in src/app.py (CORSMiddleware) to process requests before handlers.
Pagination is implemented for course listings using the Paginator class in src/api/courses/router.py.
The project uses the ruff linter, declared in pyproject.toml under [tool.ruff].
Unit and integration tests are provided in unit_tests/ and integration_tests/ directories.
Pydantic fields include examples via Field(..., examples=[...]) in schemas such as src/api/courses/schemas.py.
Passwords are hashed using PasswordService in src/infrastructure/security/password_service.py before storage.
Database access is abstracted through repository classes, e.g., src/infrastructure/sqlalchemy/courses/repository.py.
CORS policy is configured in src/app.py with CORSMiddleware allowing all origins.
No background tasks (e.g., FastAPI BackgroundTasks) are used for long‑running operations.
The code does not use a logging library; there are no logging statements, only occasional prints (none found).
Function names describe their purpose, e.g., create_course_run, get_actual_timetable_by_id, update_course.
Public functions and class methods include type annotations for parameters and return types throughout the codebase.
Global mutable state is avoided; state is kept inside classes or request‑scoped dependencies.
Generators (yield) are not used for large sequences; data is loaded into lists directly.
Logical expressions avoid redundant comparisons, e.g., no "if x == True" patterns found.
List, dict, and set comprehensions are used instead of map/filter with lambdas throughout the project.
Some magic numbers (e.g., page_size=9 in src/api/courses/router.py) are hard‑coded instead of being constants.
Complex conditional logic is not always broken into named boolean helpers; conditions are inlined in methods like __matched.
Functions avoid mutable default arguments; defaults are None or immutable values.
Reusable operations are encapsulated in service classes, but no custom decorators for reuse are defined.
Tests use pytest fixtures and parametrization (e.g., test_incorrect_email in unit_tests/domain/auth).
Async functions do not call blocking code like time.sleep or requests; all I/O uses async libraries.
Parallel async operations are not combined with asyncio.gather or create_task in the current code.
Secrets are loaded from environment variables via src/config.py; no hard‑coded passwords or keys.
A Dockerfile for production exists (Dockerfile.production) and builds the application.
docker‑compose files (dev.docker-compose.yaml, test.docker-compose.yaml) define multiple services (postgres, redis).
The project follows a modular folder structure with separate packages for api, domain, services, infrastructure, etc.
Business logic resides in service classes (e.g., src/services/course_run/command_service.py) separate from FastAPI routers.
